headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) Joining NVIDIA’s Halos Physical AI Safety Ecosystem
summary: Lattice Semiconductor announced earlier this month that it joined the NVIDIA Halos AI Systems Inspection Lab ecosystem, the first ANAB-accredited inspection lab for AI-driven physical systems, to help build Holoscan Sensor Bridge-based designs for safer, low-latency physical AI at the edge. This move ties Lattice’s low-power FPGA expertise directly into NVIDIA’s emerging physical AI safety stack for vehicles and robotics, potentially deepening its role in next-generation edge AI...

headline: Huawei's new AI chip find favour with ByteDance, Alibaba which plan to place orders, sources say
summary: Customer testing of Huawei's new AI chip, designed to challenge Nvidia in the China market, has gone well and big tech giants including ByteDance and Alibaba ‌plan to place orders, two people familiar with the matter said.  The development marks a milestone for Huawei.  Despite a ‌government campaign to encourage the use of domestic semiconductors, the Shenzhen-based firm struggled to persuade big tech firms in the private sector to adopt its ​current flagship chip, the Ascend 910C, in large quantities, industry sources have previously said.

content.title: Huawei's new AI chip find favour with ByteDance, Alibaba which plan to place orders, sources say
content.summary: Customer testing of Huawei's new AI chip, designed to challenge Nvidia in the China market, has gone well and big tech giants including ByteDance and Alibaba ‌plan to place orders, two people familiar with the matter said.  The development marks a milestone for Huawei.  Despite a ‌government campaign to encourage the use of domestic semiconductors, the Shenzhen-based firm struggled to persuade big tech firms in the private sector to adopt its ​current flagship chip, the Ascend 910C, in large quantities, industry sources have previously said.

headline: NVIDIA Faces Crypto Revenue Class Action And Questions For Future Disclosures
summary: A California federal judge has certified a class action lawsuit against NVIDIA and CEO Jensen Huang. The suit alleges material misrepresentation of over $1 billion in crypto mining GPU revenue in the gaming segment during 2017 to 2018. The case, revived after security and regulatory reviews and Supreme Court action, has now advanced to trial. The action introduces legal and reputational risk around NVIDIA's historical disclosures on crypto related revenue. NVIDIA, ticker NasdaqGS:NVDA, now...

content.title: NVIDIA Faces Crypto Revenue Class Action And Questions For Future Disclosures

The suit alleges material misrepresentation of over $1 billion in crypto mining GPU revenue in the gaming segment during 2017 to 2018. The case, revived after security and regulatory reviews and Supreme Court action, has now advanced to trial. headline: Super Micro Computer Hit With Securities Fraud Lawsuit Amid Alleged China Sales Cover-Up And Nvidia Chip Smuggling Scandal
summary: Super Micro Computer is facing a class-action lawsuit. The server maker is accused of misleading investors about exposure to China and compliance risks tied to the sale of export-controlled Nvidia Corp. chips. Investors Allege Hidden China Sales And Compliance Failures On Wednesday, shareholders claimed SMCI overstated its growth prospects by failing to disclose that a significant portion of its server sales were linked to China, potentially in violation of U.S. export controls, Reuters reported

content.title: Super Micro Computer Hit With Securities Fraud Lawsuit Amid Alleged China Sales Cover-Up And Nvidia Chip Smuggling Scandal
